name: National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific
tagline: A sacred resting place inside an extinct volcanic crater — free to visit, with sweeping views of Honolulu and a humbling sense of history.
parking: Free parking inside the crater. Drive up Puowaina Drive
bestTime: Early morning for cooler temps and fewer visitors. Combine with a Tantalus Drive loop
talkStory: The Hawaiian name Puowaina means 'Hill of Sacrifice' — ancient Hawaiians used this crater for ceremonies long before it became a military cemetery in 1949. Over 53,000 veterans rest here, including 27 Medal of Honor recipients and war correspondent Ernie Pyle. The Courts of the Missing list 28,000+ names of service members never recovered — volunteers still work to identify remains using modern DNA technology. The mosaic murals in the memorial building depict key Pacific and Korean War battles.
